addptr.description: Computes a new pointer by adding an element offset to the base pointer. The offset is in elements (not bytes).
addptr.one_liner: Add an element offset to a !pto.ptr

alloc_multi_tile.description: Produces a `!pto.multi_tile_buf<S, count=N>` value owning N physical slots of the inner tile shape `S`. In the default pipeline (`--pto-level=level1|level2`) no `addr` operand is given and the physical addresses of the N slots are decided by PTOPlanMemory. Under `--pto-level=level3` the local memory is owned by the upper-level caller and PTOPlanMemory does not run, so an explicit base address operand `$addr` is required (mirroring `pto.alloc_tile`). The N slots are laid out contiguously as the region `[addr, addr + N * slotBytes)` with slot `k` at `addr + k * slotBytes`, where `slotBytes` is the byte footprint of one slot tile (`product(shape) * element_size`). The caller is responsible for having reserved `N * slotBytes` bytes at `addr`. `valid_row` / `valid_col` operands follow the same semantics as `pto.alloc_tile`: required when the inner tile's valid shape is `?`, absent when both valid dims are static.
tags: Multi-buffer tile allocation and slot selection

barrier_sync.description: A convenience barrier that specifies a SyncOpType instead of PIPE. The lowering pass maps the op type to the corresponding hardware pipe and emits `pto.barrier`.
barrier_sync.one_liner: High-level barrier mapped from SyncOpType to PIPE

bind_tile.description: Wraps a memref with PTO metadata (valid dimensions and config).
bind_tile.one_liner: Binds metadata and implicitly casts layout
tags: Multi-buffer tile allocation and slot selection

bitcast.description: Returns a tile buffer view with a different element type (dtype) while reusing the same underlying storage as the source. This op is a metadata/config rewrite only (no data movement).
bitcast.one_liner: SSA dtype reinterpretation of a tile buffer view (aliases src storage)
tags: Multi-buffer tile allocation and slot selection

castptr.description: Performs an explicit pointer-domain cast. Supported cases: - integer -> !pto.ptr<elem, space> - !pto.ptr<elem, space> -> integer - !pto.ptr<srcElem, space> -> !pto.ptr<dstElem, space> - memref<..., space> -> !pto.ptr<elem, space> (extract the aligned base ptr) Pointer-to-pointer casts must stay within the same PTO memory space. Cross space casts such as gm <-> ub are rejected by the verifier.
castptr.one_liner: Cast between integer and !pto.ptr, or between !pto.ptr types

cmo.cacheinvalid.description: Cache maintenance operation for the specified memory space. The whole-cache form lowers to PTO-ISA's two-argument `dcci((__gm__ void*)0, cache_line_t::ENTIRE_DATA_CACHE)`. The single-cache-line form carries the GM payload address selected by the producer DSL/user. When it is required for a cacheable scalar path, it lowers to `dcci(addr, cache_line_t::SINGLE_CACHE_LINE)`. When it only marks a non-cacheable payload for signal release analysis, MemoryConsistency may consume it as marker-only IR and erase it before EmitC lowering. MemoryConsistency interprets both forms as explicit CMO boundaries on producer release and consumer acquire paths. It also uses the addressed form to match a TNotify payload against pending MTE2, MTE3, FIX, or scalar GM accesses before deciding which pipe drains are required. Precise coverage of the selected line is the producer DSL/user's responsibility in this phase.
cmo.cacheinvalid.one_liner: Perform cache maintenance for a memory space

comm.build_async_session.one_liner: Build an async DMA session handle for TPUT_ASYNC/TGET_ASYNC

comm.tbroadcast.one_liner: Broadcast local GM data to all group members

comm.test_async_event.one_liner: Non-blocking completion test for an async DMA event

comm.tgather.one_liner: Gather remote GM data from a communication group

comm.tget.one_liner: Synchronous remote read from remote GM to local GM

comm.tget_async.one_liner: Asynchronous remote read from remote GM to local GM

comm.tnotify.one_liner: Send a signal notification to remote GM

comm.tput.one_liner: Synchronous remote write from local GM to remote GM

comm.tput_async.one_liner: Asynchronous remote write from local GM to remote GM

comm.treduce.one_liner: Reduce remote GM data from a communication group

comm.tscatter.one_liner: Scatter local GM data to a communication group

comm.twait.one_liner: Block until signal(s) satisfy a comparison

comm.wait_async_event.one_liner: Block until an async DMA event completes

dcci.description: Lowers to the `llvm.hivm.DCCI*` intrinsic family. The pointer address space selects the intrinsic variant: GM pointers use `DCCI` / `DCCI.DST`; UB pointers use `DCCI.UB` / `DCCI.DST.UB`.
dcci.one_liner: Issue a low-level DCCI cache maintenance operation

declare_eventid_array.description: Declares a local array used to store dynamic event ids in manual sync code. This is a frontend/EmitC convenience feature and is not intended to participate in PlanMemory or automatic synchronization passes.
declare_eventid_array.one_liner: Declare a manual-only local event-id array

declare_global.one_liner: Declare a GlobalTensor descriptor whose address is assigned later

declare_local_array.description: Lowers to a plain C++ stack variable like `T a[d1][d2]...;`. The element type is restricted to scalars (integer / float); no nested aggregates. This op does not participate in PTO memory planning. The resulting array's address is determined by the host C++ compiler.

declare_tile.one_liner: Declare a tile value whose address will be assigned later

declare_tile_memref.description: Internal lowering op used by PTOViewToMemref. This op does not allocate storage; it only provides a memref-typed SSA handle so later passes can attach tile metadata through pto.bind_tile before the address is filled by pipe operations such as pto.tpop.
declare_tile_memref.one_liner: Internal memref placeholder for a tile whose address is assigned later

dsb.description: Lowers to `llvm.hivm.DSB(i64)`. The memory domain maps directly to the CCE `mem_dsb_t` enum values: `ALL`, `DDR`, `UB`, and `SEQ`.
dsb.one_liner: Issue a low-level DSB memory barrier

eventid_array_get.one_liner: Read one element from a manual event-id array

eventid_array_set.one_liner: Write one element into a manual event-id array

fence.barrier_all.description: Memory fence for a visibility scope. Around signal/payload communication it can be used as the producer-side publish barrier or the consumer-side acquire barrier depending on placement. `scope = gm` lowers to `dsb(DSB_DDR)`.
fence.barrier_all.one_liner: All-direction memory fence for a visibility scope

fusion_region.description: `pto.fusion_region` wraps one already-scheduled fusion group inside the current function and exposes explicit escaping values as results. The body is a lightweight single-block container and may implicitly capture surrounding SSA values from the parent scope.
fusion_region.one_liner: Structured region container for one scheduled tile fusion group

get_block_idx.description: Returns the linear index of the current compute unit (CUBE Core) within the task. The return value is in the range [0, BlockNum - 1].
get_block_idx.one_liner: Get the current block index (core ID).

get_block_num.description: Returns the total number of compute units (Blocks) configured for the current task.
get_block_num.one_liner: Get the total number of blocks (cores).

get_buf.description: `pto.get_buf` participates in a buffer-id based ordering model. Operations mapped to the same pipe and guarded by the same buffer-id are enforced to execute in program order relative to other mapped pipes using the same buffer-id. Like `pto.record_event` / `pto.wait_event`, this op accepts a high-level op type endpoint (`pipe_event_type` / `sync_op_type`) instead of a concrete pipe name. This op is intended to be lowered to the CCEC builtin intrinsic `get_buf`.
get_buf.one_liner: Acquire a buffer-id token for a sync op type (A5)

get_buf_dyn.description: Dynamic variant of `pto.get_buf` where the buffer-id is provided as an SSA value (index) instead of a static integer attribute. This enables runtime-computed buf_id patterns such as SIMT ping-pong buffering (e.g. `iter & 1`).

get_subblock_idx.description: Returns the ID of the current compute unit (Vector Core). The return value is in the range [0, 1].
get_subblock_idx.one_liner: Get the current vector core ID.

get_subblock_num.description: Returns the total number of vector compute units.
get_subblock_num.one_liner: Get the number of vector cores.

get_tensor_view_dim.description: Returns the size of the given dimension of a logical tensor view. This op accepts either !pto.tensor_view or the memref it is lowered to. IR (tensor_view form): %dim_size = pto.get_tensor_view_dim %tv, %dim_index : !pto.tensor_view, index -> index IR (memref form, after lowering): %dim_size = pto.get_tensor_view_dim %mr, %dim_index : memref<...>, index -> index
get_tensor_view_dim.one_liner: Get the size of a dimension of a tensor_view.

get_tensor_view_stride.description: Returns the stride, measured in elements, of the given dimension of a logical tensor view. This op accepts either !pto.tensor_view or the memref it is lowered to.
get_tensor_view_stride.one_liner: Get the stride of a dimension of a tensor_view.

get_validshape.description: Reads the current runtime valid_row/valid_col metadata from a tile_buf handle. This op observes prior set_validshape updates and returns the current values as index-typed SSA values.
get_validshape.one_liner: Read runtime valid row/col metadata from a tile
tags: Multi-buffer tile allocation and slot selection

import_reserved_buffer.one_liner: Import a peer reserved local consumer slot buffer

inttoptr.description: Converts an i64 byte address to a global pointer of the requested element type. This is an escape hatch for explicit byte-address arithmetic and cross-element-type pointer reinterpretation. To keep byte-address-derived pointers from escaping into general pointer IR, the result may only be used directly as the pointer operand of `pto.load_scalar` or `pto.store_scalar`.
inttoptr.one_liner: Convert an i64 byte address to a global pointer

keep.one_liner: Preserve one scalar payload across adjacent SIMT VF entries

ldg.description: Load a scalar or packed vector value from GM (Global Memory). Supported value types: - Scalar: i8/i16/i32/i64, f16/bf16/f32/f64, fp8/hif8 - Packed: vector<2xf16>, vector<2xbf16>, vector<2xf32>, vector<2xf8E4M3FN>, vector<2xf8E5M2>, vector<2xi8>, vector<2xi16>, vector<2xi32>, and !pto.hif8x2 The offset operand is an element-level ``index`` (not byte offset). For ``!pto.ptr<vector<2xf32>, gm>``, offset 1 advances by 8 bytes (2 × sizeof(f32)). The effective address for ``vector<2xf32>`` must satisfy 8-byte alignment.
ldg.one_liner: Load one element from GM with cache controls.

load.one_liner: Load one scalar element from a VPTO pointer-like operand.

load_scalar.one_liner: Load a single scalar element from a pointer at offset.

local_array_get.description: Lowers to a scalar snapshot initialized from `a[i0][i1]...[iN-1]` in the emitted C++. This preserves SSA value semantics if the array is mutated later. The number of indices must equal the array's rank.
local_array_get.one_liner: Read one element from a stack-local array

local_array_set.description: Lowers to `a[i0][i1]...[iN-1] = v;` in the emitted C++. The number of indices must equal the array's rank.
local_array_set.one_liner: Write one element into a stack-local array

make_prefetch_async_context.description: Materializes a first-class PTO IR `PrefetchAsyncContext` value from a GM workspace pointer.
make_prefetch_async_context.one_liner: Build a PrefetchAsyncContext from GM workspace.

make_tensor_view.one_liner: Wrap a pointer as a tensor_view descriptor (no allocation, no copy).

materialize_tile.description: Bridges the memref-based memory-planning/sync IR back into tile-buffer handle IR immediately before EmitC. The source remains the planned memref address, while the result carries the tile type, valid dims, and config expected by tile operations and C++ tile codegen.
materialize_tile.one_liner: Materializes a tile buffer handle from a planned memref
tags: Multi-buffer tile allocation and slot selection

mem_bar.description: Low-level memory ordering barrier that lowers to one of the `llvm.hivm.mem.bar.*` intrinsics exposed by Bisheng.

mgather.description: Two destination forms are supported: - **GM -> UB (VEC)**: `mem` is a GM table, `idx` is a UB index tile, and `dst` is a UB (`loc=vec`) tile. This is the default path. - **GM -> L1 (MAT)**: `dst` is an L1 / cube (`loc=mat`) tile in NZ layout, `idx` is supplied as a **GM** tensor (the cube core cannot read UB on A5), and `Coalesce::Elem` additionally requires a contiguous **GM `scratch`** workspace used to stage the gathered elements into NZ layout before the bulk GM -> L1 copy. `Coalesce::Row` needs no scratch. The path is selected by `dst` being a `loc=mat` tile and mirrors the pto-isa `MGATHER` GM -> L1 overloads.
mgather.one_liner: Gather-load elements from memory into a tile using per-element indices (tile world, ins/outs).

mscatter.one_liner: Scatter-store elements from a tile into memory using per-element indices (tile world, ins/outs).

multi_tile_get.description: Returns a single-slot view of a `multi_tile_buf`. The result `tile_buf` must equal the source's per-slot type; the only thing this op selects is which of the N physical slots is referred to. The slot index is an `index` value in `[0, count)`. If it is a constant, the verifier checks the range. If it is a runtime SSA value, downstream sync analysis treats this use as a slot-indexed dynamic access; ptoas does NOT rewrite the user expression into `iv mod N`. This op is metadata-only (no data movement); the lowering merely annotates the underlying memref view with the slot index for PlanMemory / sync / EnableBufferSelect to consume.
multi_tile_get.one_liner: Pick one physical slot of a multi_tile_buf
tags: Multi-buffer tile allocation and slot selection

partition_view.description: Captures a specific calculation region from a large view. It carries offsets (where to read) and sizes (how much to read). IR Example: %1 = pto.partition_view %0, offsets=[...], sizes=[...] : !pto.tensor_view -> !pto.partition_tensor_view
partition_view.one_liner: Partition a tensor view into a smaller logical view (logical slicing).

pointer_cast.one_liner: Casts an integer address to a MemRef with optional valid dims

print.description: Debug print op. First argument is the format string (string attribute), second is a scalar value. Format in IR: `pto.print ins("format", %scalar : type(%scalar))`
print.one_liner: Print debug: format string (attribute) and scalar value.

ptrtoint.description: Converts a global pointer value to an i64 byte address. If the source pointer is produced by `pto.addptr`, lowering materializes the addptr offset as an explicit byte offset before converting to EmitC.
ptrtoint.one_liner: Convert a global pointer to a byte address

record_event.description: Records an event from a source operation type to a destination operation type. Will be lowered to `pto.set_flag` based on op-to-pipe mapping.
record_event.one_liner: Record an event for synchronization (High Level)

reserve_buffer.one_liner: Reserve a local consumer slot buffer

resume.one_liner: Restore one scalar payload preserved by a previous SIMT VF entry

rls_buf.description: Releases the previously acquired buffer-id token for the given op type (mapped to a concrete pipe). This op is intended to be lowered to the CCEC builtin intrinsic `rls_buf`.
rls_buf.one_liner: Release a buffer-id token for a sync op type (A5)

rls_buf_dyn.description: Dynamic variant of `pto.rls_buf` where the buffer-id is provided as an SSA value (index) instead of a static integer attribute.

set_flag_dyn.one_liner: Set synchronization flag between pipes (dynamic event id)

set_quant_scalar.description: Producer-side scalar quantization configuration for fixpipe TPUSH. Binds an f32 scalar quant payload to a logical pipe id. This binding persists until overwritten by another set_quant_scalar with the same id. Used by scalar quant modes: deqf16_scalar, req8_scalar, qf322b8_pre_scalar, qf322f16_pre_scalar, qf322bf16_pre_scalar, qs322bf16_pre_scalar, qf322hif8_pre_scalar, qf322fp8_pre_scalar.
set_quant_scalar.one_liner: Set scalar quantization parameters for fixpipe producer

set_quant_vector.description: Producer-side vector quantization configuration for fixpipe TPUSH. Binds a vector quant payload (scaling tile) to a logical pipe id. The input tile must have loc=scaling. A5 consumes a direct scaling payload with an element type in the f16/bf16/f32 family; A3 consumes a packed 64-bit integer payload (i64/ui64). This binding persists until overwritten by another set_quant_vector with the same id. Used by vector quant modes: deqf16_vec, req8_vec, qf322b8_pre_vec, qs322bf16_pre_vec.
set_quant_vector.one_liner: Set vector quantization parameters for fixpipe producer

set_validshape.description: Updates the runtime valid_row/valid_col metadata on an existing dynamic tile_buf handle in place. This op does not allocate or alias a new tile handle. It is only valid for tile_buf types whose valid_row and valid_col are both dynamic (`?`), and the source must be a locally bound tile handle (for example `alloc_tile` or a view derived from it). Function arguments/results are not supported.
set_validshape.one_liner: Update valid row/col metadata in-place on a dynamic tile
tags: Multi-buffer tile allocation and slot selection

simd.tile_to_memref.description: This op is the canonical bridge marker for OP-Lib templates to expose a memref view from tile-like values while keeping external ABI as !pto.tile_buf. In tile_buf world, src is !pto.tile_buf and dst is the corresponding memref bridge type. After memref-world lowering, src may already be memref and this op remains as a marker for backend lowering (EmitC) to materialize tile data access.
simd.tile_to_memref.one_liner: Bridge cast from tile_buf to memref in OP-Lib bodies.

simt_launch.description: `pto.simt_launch` is source-level sugar for configuring SIMT VF launch dimensions and calling a function marked with `pto.simt_entry`. The textual dimension order follows the familiar launch order `<<<x, y, z>>>`; the canonical `pto.store_vfsimt_info` operation stores the same values in `z, y, x` order for the backend.
simt_launch.one_liner: Launch a SIMT entry with VF launch dimensions

slot_marker.description: Internal op materialized by `PTOViewToMemref` while lowering `pto.multi_tile_get`. It carries the slot SSA index through the memref layer so that PlanMemory, sync analysis (InsertSync / GraphSyncSolver), and the buffer-select lowering pass can identify which physical slot this memref reference touches. The op is metadata-only (no data movement, no extra address arithmetic); its result memref aliases the source memref byte-for-byte. Frontends do not produce this op directly -- use `pto.multi_tile_get` instead.
slot_marker.one_liner: Tag a memref view as referring to one slot of a multi_tile_buf

stg.description: Store a scalar or packed vector value to GM (Global Memory). Supported value types and offset/alignment semantics are the same as ``pto.ldg``. See ``pto.ldg`` for details.
stg.one_liner: Store one element to GM with cache controls.

store.one_liner: Store one scalar element to a VPTO pointer-like operand.

store_scalar.one_liner: Store a single scalar element to a pointer at offset.

strict_vecscope.description: `pto.strict_vecscope` is the strict form of `pto.vecscope`. Values used by the body must be passed explicitly through op operands and corresponding block arguments; implicit SSA capture from the surrounding scope is rejected.
strict_vecscope.one_liner: Structured VPTO vector scope with explicit captures only

subview.description: Creates a view into the source tile. - Logical subview window: defined by `offsets` + static `sizes`. - Result tile type shape: equals subview `sizes` (logical shape). - Effective subview extent: represented by inferred `valid_row/valid_col` (i.e. `valid_shape`), clipped by subview `sizes`. - Lowering keeps parent physical stride/base semantics for non-compact access, while IR type exposes logical subview size for readability.
subview.one_liner: Create a tile subview from a parent tile (logical size + valid dims).
tags: Multi-buffer tile allocation and slot selection

sync.set.description: Sets a synchronization signal on the specified pipeline stage. Corresponds to `ffts_cross_core_sync` (A3) or `set_intra_block` (A5). On A3, the optional `ffts_mode` attribute selects the first argument passed to `getFFTSMsg`; if omitted, the legacy `FFTS_MODE_VAL` path is preserved.
sync.set.one_liner: Set a synchronization signal (trigger) between cube and vector.

sync.wait.description: Waits for a synchronization signal on the specified pipeline stage. Corresponds to `wait_flag_dev` (A3) or `wait_intra_block` (A5).
sync.wait.one_liner: Wait for a synchronization signal (barrier) between cube and vector.

syncall.description: Models `pto::SYNCALL`. Supported IR forms: - hard sync: no operands, `mode = hard` - soft AIV-only sync: `gm_workspace`, `ub_workspace`, optional `used_cores` - soft AIC-only sync: `gm_workspace`, `l1_workspace`, optional `used_cores` - soft mixed sync: `gm_workspace`, `ub_workspace`, `l1_workspace`, optional `used_cores` The verifier mirrors the input-form constraints exposed by the public `pto-isa` API.

syncthreads.one_liner: Synchronize all workitems in a SIMT VF entry

tabs.one_liner: Elementwise absolute value of a tile

tadd.description: For each element (i, j): dst[i,j] = src0[i,j] + src1[i,j].

taddc.description: For each element (i, j): dst[i,j] = src0[i,j] + src1[i,j] + src2[i,j].

tadds.description: For each element (i, j): dst[i,j] = src[i,j] + scalar. Tile operands are tilebuffers; scalar is a builtin scalar type (e.g. f32).
tadds.one_liner: Elementwise add a scalar to a tile

talloc.one_liner: Bind a declared GlobalTensor descriptor to the next producer FIFO slot

talloc_to_aic.one_liner: Frontend V2C producer alloc of a GlobalTensor FIFO entry

talloc_to_aiv.one_liner: Frontend C2V producer alloc of a GlobalTensor FIFO entry

tand.one_liner: TAND(dst, src0, src1) bitwise-and on tiles

tands.one_liner: TANDS(dst, src, scalar) bitwise-and tile with scalar

tassign.description: Rebinds an existing tile handle to a new address. This op is intended for manual synchronization workflows (for example, rebinding inside loops while keeping the tile declaration outside the loop).
tassign.one_liner: Rebind a declared tile handle to a runtime address

tci.one_liner: Generate contiguous integer sequence into dst tile.

tcmp.one_liner: Compare two tiles and write a packed predicate mask

tcmps.one_liner: Compare scalar value against a tile and write a packed predicate mask

tcolargmax.one_liner: Reduce each column to the row index of the maximum element

tcolargmin.one_liner: Reduce each column to the row index of the minimum element

tcolexpand.one_liner: Broadcast src(0, j) to all rows in column j

tcolexpandadd.one_liner: Column-wise broadcast add: add a per-column scalar vector src1 to src0

tcolexpanddiv.one_liner: Column-wise broadcast divide: divide each element of src0 by a per-column scalar vector src1

tcolexpandexpdif.one_liner: Column-wise broadcast expdif: compute exp(src0 - src1) using a per-column scalar vector src1

tcolexpandmax.one_liner: Column-wise broadcast max: take max(src0, src1) using a per-column scalar vector src1

tcolexpandmin.one_liner: Column-wise broadcast min: take min(src0, src1) using a per-column scalar vector src1

tcolexpandmul.one_liner: Column-wise broadcast multiply: multiply each element of src0 by a per-column scalar vector src1

tcolexpandsub.one_liner: Column-wise broadcast subtract: subtract a per-column scalar vector src1 from src0

tcolmax.one_liner: Reduce each column by taking the maximum across rows

tcolmin.one_liner: Reduce each column by taking the minimum across rows

tcolprod.one_liner: Reduce each column by multiplying across rows

tcolsum.one_liner: Reduce each column by summing across rows (tilebuf-based, explicit tmp)

tconcat.description: Semantics (per row): dst[:, 0:validCol0] = src0 dst[:, validCol0:validCol0+validCol1] = src1 Constraints are implementation-defined; see verifier for current checks.
tconcat.one_liner: TCONCAT: Concatenate two tiles along the column dimension.

tconcatidx.description: Indexed form of tconcat. Takes two additional index tiles (src0Idx, src1Idx) that specify the number of columns to copy from each source on a per-row basis. Semantics (per row): idx0_num = src0Idx[row, 0] idx1_num = src1Idx[row, 0] copy from src0: min(idx0_num, src0_valid_col, dst_valid_col) columns copy from src1: min(idx1_num, src1_valid_col, dst_valid_col - copied_from_src0) columns
tconcatidx.one_liner: TCONCAT (indexed): Concatenate two tiles along the column dimension with per-row index control.

tcvt.one_liner: Elementwise type conversion with optional saturation mode (tilebuf, DPS)

tdequant.description: Converts an integer (int8 or int16) src tile to float32, applying per-row scale and offset parameters held in the scale and offset tiles. Formula: dst[i][j] = (float(src[i][j]) - offset[i][0]) * scale[i][0] The scale and offset tiles are row-vectors: one f32 value per row is broadcast across all columns of that row. DPS form: pto.tdequant ins(%src, %scale, %offset : <src_type>, <scale_type>, <offset_type>) outs(%dst : <dst_type>)
tdequant.one_liner: TDEQUANT: Dequantize an integer tile using per-row scale and offset tiles.

tdiv.description: For each element (i, j): dst[i,j] = src0[i,j] / src1[i,j]. Division-by-zero behavior is target-defined.
tdiv.one_liner: Elementwise division of two tiles (tilebuf, DPS)

tdivs.one_liner: Elementwise division with a scalar (tilebuf, DPS)

tensor_view_addr.description: Returns the address view carried by a `tensor_view` or `partition_tensor_view` value. The result may be either a memref view or a typed PTO pointer, depending on the requested destination type. In authoring-form IR this op preserves the descriptor-style surface; during view-to-memref lowering it collapses to the underlying memref value or to a memref-derived pointer. This op may also accept a memref operand after earlier view lowering, in which case it behaves as an identity marker and is removed by lowering.
tensor_view_addr.one_liner: Extract address from a tensor view.

tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)
PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

textract.one_liner: Extract sub-tile window from src into dst (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

textract_fp.one_liner: Extract acc tile window into dst using fp/scaling tile (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tfillpad.one_liner: Copy src into dst and fill padded elements using dst PadVal (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tfillpad_expand.one_liner: Copy src into dst and fill padded elements using dst PadVal, allowing dst to be larger (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tfillpad_inplace.one_liner: In-place fill padding on shared backing storage: src supplies valid-region bounds, dst supplies target pad bounds (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tfmod.one_liner: Elementwise fmod/remainder of two tiles (tilebuf, DPS)

tfmods.one_liner: Elementwise fmod/remainder with a scalar (tilebuf, DPS)

tfree.one_liner: Release the current consumer slot for a pipe

tfree_from_aic.one_liner: Frontend C2V consumer free in Vector kernel

tfree_from_aiv.one_liner: Frontend V2C consumer free in Cube kernel

tgather.one_liner: Gather/select elements using index+tmp, scalar-compare+tmp, or a mask pattern (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tgatherb.one_liner: Gather elements using byte offsets (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tgemv.one_liner: PTO matrix-vector multiplication operation (optional bias), destination-style (DPS) in tile world.

tgemv.acc.one_liner: PTO matrix-vector multiplication accumulate operation, destination-style (DPS, no bias).

tgemv.bias.one_liner: GEMV with bias add (tile world, ins/outs).

tgemv.mx.one_liner: Mixed-precision GEMV with scale tiles (tile world, ins/outs).

tgemv.mx.acc.one_liner: Mixed-precision GEMV accumulate with scale tiles (tile world, ins/outs).

tgemv.mx.bias.one_liner: Mixed-precision GEMV with bias and scale tiles (tile world, ins/outs).

tget_scale_addr.one_liner: Bind a scaling tile to the scaled address of a source tile.

tgetval.one_liner: Read a single element from tile-like src at offset into a scalar (tile world, ins/outs).

threadfence.one_liner: Issue a SIMT workitem memory fence

threadfence_block.one_liner: Issue a SIMT block-scoped workitem memory fence

tile_buf_addr.description: Returns the address view of the data region of a `tile_buf` value. The result may be either a memref view or a typed PTO pointer, depending on the requested destination type. Memref results use the tile's static shape and address space. This op is emitted by TileLang DSL templates and resolved by the FoldTileBufIntrinsics pass after inlining. Hand-written `.pto` may also use it directly on the memref result of `pto.bind_tile` / lowered `pto.alloc_tile`.

tile_valid_cols.description: Returns the valid column count (v_col) of a `tile_buf` as an `index` value. When the tile_buf has a static v_col, FoldTileBufIntrinsics folds this into `arith.constant`. When v_col is dynamic (`?`), the fold resolves it to the runtime index value carried by the tile_buf.
tile_valid_cols.one_liner: Extract valid column count from a tile_buf.

tile_valid_rows.description: Returns the valid row count (v_row) of a `tile_buf` as an `index` value. When the tile_buf has a static v_row, FoldTileBufIntrinsics folds this into `arith.constant`. When v_row is dynamic (`?`), the fold resolves it to the runtime index value carried by the tile_buf.
tile_valid_rows.one_liner: Extract valid row count from a tile_buf.

tinsert.one_liner: Insert src sub-tile into dst at (indexRow, indexCol) (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tinsert_fp.one_liner: Insert acc tile window into dst using fp/scaling tile (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tload.description: Loads data from a high-dimensional logical partition view into a 2D physical tile buffer. Constraint: The product of partition view sizes must match the product of tile buffer valid dims. DPS form: pto.tload ins(%partition_view) outs(%tile_buf)
tload.one_liner: PTO data load operation (Dimension Collapse: PartitionView -> TileBuf).

tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tlrelu.one_liner: Leaky ReLU with a scalar slope (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tmatmul.one_liner: PTO matrix multiplication operation (optional bias), destination-style (DPS) in tile world.

tmatmul.acc.one_liner: PTO matrix multiplication accumulate operation, destination-style (DPS, no bias).

tmatmul.bias.one_liner: Matmul with bias add (tile world, ins/outs).

tmatmul.mx.bias.one_liner: Matmul mx with bias (tile world, ins/outs).

tmax.one_liner: Elementwise maximum of two tiles (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tmaxs.one_liner: Elementwise max of a tile and a scalar (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tmin.one_liner: Elementwise minimum of two tiles (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tmins.one_liner: Elementwise minimum of a tile and a scalar (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tmov.fp.one_liner: TMOV_FP: move/convert using fp (scaling) tile (tilebuf, DPS)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tmrgsort.one_liner: TMRGSORT: Merge sort (format1: ins(src, blockLen) outs(dst); format2: ins(2 to 4 srcs, tmp) outs(dst, executed)).
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tmul.one_liner: TMUL: Elementwise multiply of two tiles.
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tmuls.one_liner: TMULS: Elementwise multiply a tile by a scalar.

tneg.one_liner: TNEG: Elementwise negation of a tile.

tnot.one_liner: TNOT: Elementwise bitwise NOT of a tile.

tor.one_liner: TOR: Elementwise bitwise OR of two tiles.

tors.one_liner: TORS: Elementwise bitwise OR of a tile and a scalar.

tpartadd.one_liner: TPARTADD: Partial elementwise add with implementation-defined handling of mismatched valid regions.

tpartargmax.one_liner: Partial elementwise max with value and index tiles.

tpartargmin.one_liner: Partial elementwise min with value and index tiles.

tpartmax.one_liner: Partial elementwise max with implementation-defined handling of mismatched valid regions.

tpartmin.one_liner: Partial elementwise min with implementation-defined handling of mismatched valid regions.

tpartmul.one_liner: Partial elementwise mul with implementation-defined handling of mismatched valid regions.

tpop.one_liner: Bind a declared consumer tile to the next FIFO slot

tpop_from_aic.one_liner: Frontend C2V consumer pop in Vector kernel

tpop_from_aiv.one_liner: Frontend V2C consumer pop in Cube kernel

tpow.description: For each element (i, j): dst[i,j] = pow(base[i,j], exp[i,j]). The `tmp` tile is backend scratch only used by the floating-point lowering path: it is required when the element type is floating-point or when `precisionType = high_precision`, and must be omitted for the pure integer path.
tpow.one_liner: TPOW: Elementwise power with both base and exponent as tiles.
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOW: elementwise tile-tile power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tpows.description: For each element (i, j): dst[i,j] = pow(src[i,j], scalar). The `tmp` tile is backend scratch only used by the floating-point lowering path: required when the element type is floating-point or when `precisionType = high_precision`, and must be omitted for the pure integer path.
tpows.one_liner: TPOWS: Elementwise power with a scalar exponent.
tags: PTOOps.td  (TPOWS: elementwise tile-scalar power, DPS, tmp scratch optional)

tprefetch.description: Prefetches data from a global partition view into a temporary local tile buffer. Unlike most PTO intrinsics, TPREFETCH does not carry implicit TSYNC(wait_events...) semantics in PTO-ISA. DPS form: pto.tprefetch ins(%src) outs(%dst)
tprefetch.one_liner: Prefetch global data into a local tile buffer.

tprefetch_async.one_liner: Asynchronously prefetch global data into L2 cache via SDMA CMO.

tprelu.description: DPS form: tile buf: pto.tprelu ins(%src0, %src1, %tmp) outs(%dst)
tprelu.one_liner: TPRELU: Elementwise PReLU (parametric ReLU) with a per-element slope tile.

tprint.description: pto-isa overloads support `TPRINT(src)` and `TPRINT<Format>(src, tmp)`. The optional tmp operand is used when printing Mat/Acc tiles through a scratch GlobalTensor, while Vec tiles and GlobalTensors can be printed without tmp.
tprint.one_liner: TPRINT: Print the contents of a Tile or GlobalTensor for debugging purposes directly from device code.

tpush.one_liner: Push tile data through a unified pipe handle

tpush_to_aic.one_liner: Frontend V2C producer push from Vector to Cube

tpush_to_aiv.one_liner: Frontend C2V producer push from Cube to Vector

tquant.description: Converts src tile elements to a lower-precision format using the scaling parameters held in the fp tile, writing results into dst. For INT8_ASYM an additional offset tile must be supplied in ins. The optional tmp tile is the scratch buffer used by the A2/A3 TQUANT_IMPL row-wise broadcast (the fp->s32 conversion reuses it). On pto-isa builds where the 4-arg TQUANT overload routes through the fixed TMP_UB_OFFSET scratch area, supplying tmp selects the tmp-aware 5-arg overload and avoids the fixed-address collision. PTOAS auto-synthesizes this scratch tile for A2/A3 only. On A5 the tmp operand is accepted as a placeholder and does not add extra shape or memory-planning constraints. DPS form (INT8_SYM): pto.tquant ins(%src, %fp : <src_type>, <fp_type>) outs(%dst : <dst_type>) {quant_type = #pto<quant_type INT8_SYM>} DPS form (INT8_SYM, with tmp): pto.tquant ins(%src, %fp : <src_type>, <fp_type>) outs(%dst, %tmp : <dst_type>, <tmp_type>) {quant_type = #pto<quant_type INT8_SYM>} DPS form (INT8_ASYM): pto.tquant ins(%src, %fp, %offset : <src_type>, <fp_type>, <offset_type>) outs(%dst : <dst_type>) {quant_type = #pto<quant_type INT8_ASYM>} DPS form (INT8_ASYM, with tmp): pto.tquant ins(%src, %fp, %offset : <src_type>, <fp_type>, <offset_type>) outs(%dst, %tmp : <dst_type>, <tmp_type>) {quant_type = #pto<quant_type INT8_ASYM>}
tquant.one_liner: TQUANT: Quantize a tile using a scaling (fp) tile.

tquant.mx.description: A5-only MX quantization form. Quantizes `src` into `dst` and additionally materializes the auxiliary `exp`, `max`, and `scaling` tiles required by PTO-ISA MX quantization. DPS form: pto.tquant.mx ins(%src : <src_type>) outs(%dst, %exp, %max, %scaling : <dst_type>, <exp_type>, <max_type>, <scaling_type>) {quant_type = #pto<quant_type MXFP8>} pto.tquant.mx ins(%src : <src_type>) outs(%dst, %exp, %max, %scaling, %exp_zz : <dst_type>, <exp_type>, <max_type>, <scaling_type>, <exp_type>) {quant_type = #pto<quant_type MXFP8>, storeMode = #pto<vec_store_mode nz>}
tquant.mx.one_liner: TQUANT MX: Quantize a vec tile into MXFP8/MXFP4 and materialize exp/max/scaling tiles.

trandom.one_liner: Generate random values into dst tile using key/counter words.

trap.description: Inserts a trap to stop execution. No arguments, no results. Format in IR: pto.trap

trecip.one_liner: TRECIP: Elementwise reciprocal of a tile.

trelu.one_liner: TRELU: Elementwise ReLU of a tile.

trem.one_liner: TREM: Elementwise remainder of two tiles.

trems.one_liner: TREMS: Elementwise remainder with a scalar

treshape.one_liner: TRESHAPE: Reinterpret a tile buffer view (SSA; aliases src storage)
tags: PTOOps.td  (TRESHAPE: SSA view op; aliases src storage)

trowargmax.one_liner: TROWARGMAX: Reduce each row to the column index of the maximum element.

trowargmin.one_liner: TROWARGMIN: Reduce each row to the column index of the minimum element.

trowexpand.one_liner: TROWEXPAND: Broadcast the first element of each source row across the destination row.

trowexpandadd.description: pto-isa has overloads with/without tmp; on A5 the tmp operand is accepted as a placeholder and does not add extra shape constraints.
trowexpandadd.one_liner: TROWEXPANDADD: Row-wise broadcast add: add a per-row scalar vector src1 to each row of src0.

trowexpanddiv.description: pto-isa has overloads with/without tmp; on A5 the tmp operand is accepted as a placeholder and does not add extra shape constraints.
trowexpanddiv.one_liner: TROWEXPANDDIV: Row-wise broadcast divide: divide each row of src0 by a per-row scalar vector src1.

trowexpandexpdif.description: pto-isa has overloads with/without tmp; on A5 the tmp operand is accepted as a placeholder and does not add extra shape constraints.
trowexpandexpdif.one_liner: TROWEXPANDEXPDIF: Row-wise broadcast expdif with per-row scalar vector.

trowexpandmax.description: pto-isa has overloads with/without tmp; on A5 the tmp operand is accepted as a placeholder and does not add extra shape constraints.
trowexpandmax.one_liner: TROWEXPANDMAX: Row-wise broadcast max with per-row scalar vector.

trowexpandmin.description: pto-isa has overloads with/without tmp; on A5 the tmp operand is accepted as a placeholder and does not add extra shape constraints.
trowexpandmin.one_liner: TROWEXPANDMIN: Row-wise broadcast min with per-row scalar vector.

trowexpandmul.description: pto-isa has overloads with/without tmp; on A5 the tmp operand is accepted as a placeholder and does not add extra shape constraints.
trowexpandmul.one_liner: TROWEXPANDMUL: Row-wise broadcast multiply with per-row scalar vector.

trowexpandsub.description: pto-isa has overloads with/without tmp; on A5 the tmp operand is accepted as a placeholder and does not add extra shape constraints.
trowexpandsub.one_liner: TROWEXPANDSUB: Row-wise broadcast subtract: subtract a per-row scalar vector src1 from each row of src0.

trowmax.one_liner: TROWMAX: Reduce each row by taking the maximum across columns.

trowmin.one_liner: TROWMIN: Reduce each row by taking the minimum across columns.

trowprod.one_liner: TROWPROD: Reduce each row by multiplying across columns.

trowsum.one_liner: TROWSUM: Reduce each row by summing across columns.

tscatter.one_liner: TSCATTER: Scatter elements of a source tile into a destination tile using indices or a mask pattern.

tsel.description: DPS form (pto-isa TSEL requires tmp): ins(%mask, %src0, %src1, %tmp) outs(%dst)
tsel.one_liner: TSEL: Select between two tiles using a mask tile (per-element selection).

tsels.description: DPS form (pto-isa TSELS requires tmp): ins(%mask, %src, %tmp, %scalar) outs(%dst)
tsels.one_liner: TSELS: Select one of two source tiles using a scalar selectMode (global select).

tsetval.one_liner: Write a scalar value into a single element of dst at offset (tile world, ins/outs).

tshl.one_liner: TSHL: Elementwise shift-left of two tiles.

tshls.one_liner: TSHLS: Elementwise shift-left of a tile by a scalar (shift count).
tags: PTOOps.td  (add TSHLS/TSHRS TBDPS: shift-left/right by scalar)

tshr.one_liner: TSHR: Elementwise shift-right of two tiles.

tshrs.one_liner: TSHRS: Elementwise shift-right of a tile by a scalar (shift count).
tags: PTOOps.td  (add TSHLS/TSHRS TBDPS: shift-left/right by scalar)

tsort32.description: DPS form matching pto-isa overloads: (dst, src, idx) or (dst, src, idx, tmp). In PTO IR, `idx` is modeled as an input operand in `ins(...)`, while `dst` remains the sole DPS init/output buffer in `outs(...)`.
tsort32.one_liner: TSORT32: Sort fixed-size 32-element blocks using explicit input indices.

tstore.description: Stores data from a 2D physical tile buffer back to a high-dimensional logical partition view. DPS form: pto.tstore ins(%tile_buf) outs(%partition_view)

tstore_fp.one_liner: TSTORE_FP: Store an accumulator tile into global memory using a scaling (fp) tile for vector quantization parameters.

tsub.one_liner: TSUB: Elementwise subtract of two tiles.

tsubc.one_liner: TSUBC: Elementwise ternary op: src0 - src1 + src2.

tsubs.one_liner: TSUBS: Elementwise subtract a scalar from a tile.

tsubsc.one_liner: TSUBSC: Elementwise fused op: src0 - scalar + src1.

tsync.description: Lowers directly to a `TSYNC(...)` intrinsic call with the same operand list. This op is intentionally a thin bridge for explicit synchronization usage.

ttrans.description: DPS form: tile buf: pto.ttrans ins(%src, %tmp) outs(%dst) The current PTO IR form always carries a tmp operand, but PTO IR does not require tmp to match the shape or valid shape of src/dst.

ttri.one_liner: Generate a lower/upper triangular mask tile

txor.description: DPS form: without tmp: pto.txor ins(%src0, %src1) outs(%dst) with tmp: pto.txor ins(%src0, %src1, %tmp) outs(%dst)
txor.one_liner: TXOR: Elementwise bitwise XOR of two tiles.

txors.description: DPS form (pto-isa TXORS requires tmp): ins(%src, %scalar, %tmp) outs(%dst)
txors.one_liner: TXORS: Elementwise bitwise XOR of a tile and a scalar.

vecscope.description: `pto.vecscope` marks a structured vector-scope interval without overloading a dummy carrier loop with scope metadata. Lowering and emission passes may use the region boundary to preserve loop shape while treating the enclosed body as one VPTO vector interval.
vecscope.one_liner: Structured region container for one VPTO vector scope

wait_event.description: Waits for an event from a source operation type to a destination operation type. Will be lowered to `pto.wait_flag` based on op-to-pipe mapping.

wait_flag_dyn.one_liner: Wait for synchronization flag (dynamic event id)

yield.description: `pto.yield` is the dedicated terminator for `pto.fusion_region`. Its operands enumerate the tile/value set that remains externally visible after encapsulation. They define the region outputs in the same order as the parent `pto.fusion_region` results, giving downstream passes a compact summary of which internal tiles still escape the region boundary.
yield.one_liner: Terminate a pto.fusion_region and return its visible outputs
